decisions.reasoning: Urshifu-Rapid Strike threatens an immediate OHKO with Close Combat or a 2HKO with Surging Strikes. Switching to Amoonguss provides a resistance to Water-type moves and forces the opponent to deal with a bulky pivot.
Urshifu-Rapid is a major threat, and Spore provides the best chance to neutralize it immediately given Amoonguss's bulk and the opponent's likely aggressive playstyle.
Urshifu-Rapid is already asleep (SLP). Spore is the optimal play to keep it incapacitated or catch a switch-in, as Amoonguss has no offensive pressure here and Spore provides the best utility.

The opponent's Urshifu-Rapid-Strike is already asleep, and Spore is the most effective way to keep it neutralized or catch a switch-in. Since Amoonguss is not in immediate danger of being KO'd, maintaining pressure with sleep is the optimal play.
Urshifu-Rapid is already asleep based on the HP status provided, so I will re-apply Spore to ensure it stays incapacitated while it is forced to stay in or switch.

Amoonguss is threatened with an OHKO by Hurricane and cannot survive the turn. Switching to Incineroar provides a safe pivot that resists Heat Wave and threatens the opponent with Intimidate and potential chip damage.

Flare Blitz is a 2HKO on Tornadus-Therian, and since I am at full health, I can comfortably survive a Hurricane to secure the KO next turn. Staying in allows me to remove a major pivot threat without sacrificing momentum.
Flare Blitz has a high chance to KO the weakened Tornadus-Therian (47% HP) this turn, preventing it from utilizing Regenerator to heal back up.

Incineroar is guaranteed to be OHKOed by Urshifu's Close Combat, and Fake Out does not provide enough chip damage to justify staying in. Amoonguss resists Urshifu's STAB moves and can safely pivot into the attack.
Urshifu-Rapid-Strike is currently asleep (SLP). Spore is the optimal play to maintain sleep pressure and prevent the opponent from acting immediately upon waking up.

Urshifu-Rapid-Strike is currently asleep (SLP), so I should capitalize on this opportunity to keep it incapacitated or refresh the sleep timer if it wakes up, as Amoonguss is the best answer to neutralize it.

Urshifu-Rapid is already asleep, so Spore is a safe play to keep it incapacitated while I maintain positioning. Since Amoonguss is not in immediate danger of being KO'd, keeping the pressure on the opponent's active threat is the best momentum play.

Urshifu-Rapid-Strike is already asleep, so Spore is the optimal play to maintain sleep pressure or catch a switch-in if they decide to swap out.

The opponent's Incineroar is asleep, making this the perfect opportunity to use Parting Shot to lower their stats and pivot into a more offensive threat while maintaining momentum.

The opponent's Incineroar is currently asleep, providing a free turn to remove its item and deal chip damage. Knock Off is the most consistent play to gain utility while the opponent is incapacitated.
